title: Quantitative Methods 1, EBC1005/1006/1007, 2018/2019, 1st Sit Exam, Version C
description: Past exam paper for Quantitative Methods 1 covering Mathematics and Statistics.

questions.text: Consider V as a function of d. Approximate, with help of the marginal function of V(d), the change in speed if the minimum distance decreases from 200 meters to 190 meters between the trains.
Its derivative f'(x) is
Its inverse is f^{-1}(y) is

We consider the differentiable function f(x) on the domain(0,\infty). We know that f(x) is positive on the whole domain. Which property should f(x) have, so that its point elasticity is guaranteed to be positive, on the whole domain?

options.text: None of the above: the point elasticity is always negative.

questions.text: In which of the following intervals lies the price at which the demand is 5?
At a price of 9 Euro’s per liter, what is the elasticity of the demand function
In which of the following intervals lies the price at which the revenue is maximum

options.text: None of the above, since the revenue is increasing.

questions.text: What is the shape of the revenue function.

options.text: First increasing, then decreasing and convex
First increasing, then decreasing and concave

questions.text: The sales of a certain product as a function of time can be described with a logistic growth function. On t=0 the process has value 6 and derivative 4. What are the values of c1 and c2?

A farmer wants to fence off a rectangular parcel of land, with length L en width B and with an area of 1200 m2. One of the sides with length L borders on a ditch and on this side no fence is needed. One of the sides with width B borders on a forest and on this forest side the costs equal 200 guilders per linear meter. The costs corresponding to the remaining two sides equal 100 guilders per linear meter. The farmer wants to minimize the costs of this fence. Then the ratio of the length L and the width B of this rectangular parcel will be equal to
Consider the function f(x) for positive values of x. Let the elasticity of f(x) be given by E_f(x). Now let g(x) := \alpha f(x) where \alpha < 0. What do you know about the elasticity E_g(x) of g(x)?

Learning Italian is done with the function N(t)=2000(1-2^{-t}) which expresses the number of words a person knows after t years. After how many years is the number of words that one learns per year, dropped to below one.
The table below gives values for the functions f(x) and g(x) and their derivatives for certain values of x. If the function h(x) = f(g(x)), what is the value of h'(2)?
The domain of the function f(x,y) is described by
The partial derivative of the function is equal to
Let f(x,y) be a function of two variables. Which one of the following statements is true?

options.text: The function g(x,y) = f(x,y) + x has the same level curves as f(x,y).
The function g(x,y) = f(x,y) + x + y has the same level curves as f(x,y).
The function g(x,y) = f(x,y) + 1 has the same level curves as f(x,y).
The function g(x,y) = f(x,y) + e^x has the same level curves as f(x,y).

questions.text: Consider El_x f(x,y), and El_y f(x,y), the elasticities with respect to x and y of the function f(x,y). Which of the following is true about these two elasticities?
When prices can be chosen independently, the maximal profit is in the interval
When one price in both markets is charged, the maximal profit is in the interval
The function f(x,y) = 3x^3 + 9x^2y + 4y^3 - 48y has

options.text: 2 stationary points, 1 local maximum location and 1 local minimum location.
4 stationary points, 1 local maximum location, 1 local minimum location, and 2 saddle points.
4 stationary points, 2 local maximum locations and 2 local minimum locations.

questions.text: Which of the following is/are categorical variable(s)? I The modal income of teachers in the Netherlands II The types of desserts available at a restaurant III The colours of cars in a parking lot

Recent figures of the customer bases of the seven main supermarkets in the Netherlands (percentage of households visiting the supermarket in a certain week) are: Albert Heijn 63.6%, Lidl 44.9%, Jumbo 43.6%, Aldi 31.1%, Plus 17.9%, Dirk 13.0%, Coop 9.2% and Emté 6.9%. An appropriate display to compare the customer bases of different supermarkets is:
Four histograms with identical horizontal and vertical scales. All histograms refer to datasets with same number of observations and the same mean. What histogram has largest standard deviation?
Descriptive statistics of the QM1 MathEntryTest, as calculated by StatCrunch, are provided in the table. In what range falls the MathEntryTest z-score of a student getting 10 points in the test?

An automobile service station performances oil changes and tire replacements, as well as other services. Sixty percent of its customers request an oil change, 30 percent request tire replacements, and 10 percent request both. A customer requests an oil change. What is the probability this customer does not request tire replacements?

Three datasets (scores on a test for three classes), pictured as histograms (Class 1, Class 2, Class 3, from left to right) and pictured as boxplots (A, B, C). What boxplot matches with what histogram?

options.text: Class 1 = A, Class 2 = B, Class 3 = C
Class 1 = B, Class 2 = A, Class 3 = C
Class 1 = C, Class 2 = A, Class 3 = B
Class 1 = C, Class 2 = B, Class 3 = A

questions.text: Which of the following statements about any two events A and B is true?

options.text: P(A or B) = 0 implies events A and B are independent
P(A or B) = 1 implies events A and B are mutually exclusive
P(A and B) = 0 implies events A and B are independent
P(A and B) = 0 implies events A and B are mutually exclusive

questions.text: In which of the following situations is a Binomial model not an appropriate model to describe the outcome?

options.text: The number of heads in three tosses of a coin
The number of rainy days in Maastricht in a given week
The number of even faces when rolling four dice
The number of students in your tutorial group having blood type B.

questions.text: Scores on quiz part of the course assessment are distributed with a mean of 4 and a standard deviation of 0.3. Scores on the written exam part of the course assessment are independent and distributed with a mean of 24 and a standard deviation of 0.6. What are the mean and standard deviation of the final assessment score (sum of quiz and exam score)?

The amount of rainfall per month in October in Maastricht is Normally distributed with a mean of 60 cm and a standard deviation of 16 cm. Which of the following is the highest amount of rainfall Maastricht could have this October to be among the 10% driest months?

A company has 400 employees. Their mean income is €41000, and the standard deviation of their incomes is €7500, The distribution of incomes is approximately Normal. How many of the 400 employees are expected to have an income between €26000 and €48500?

A company with offices in five different countries is interested in estimating the proportion of its employees in favour of banning smoking on the office premises. It is known that the views of people from different countries on issues like smoking tend to vary due to the different local social structures. Which of the following is an appropriate sampling technique to use in this situation?
From a survey of co-workers you find that 48% of 200 have already received this year’s flu vaccine. An approximate 95% confidence interval is (0.409, 0.551). Which of the following are true?

options.text: 95% of the co-workers fall in the interval (0.409, 0.551).
We are 95% confident that the proportion of co-workers who have received this year’s flu vaccine is between 40.9% and 55.1%.
There is a 95% chance that a random selected co-worker has received the vaccine.
We are 95% confident that between 40.9% and 55.1% of the samples will have a proportion near 48%.

questions.text: The registrar’s office at a university has noticed that a large proportion of students fail to report a change of address. The registrar decides to take a random sample of 200 students and determine the number of students with the correct address on record. She finds that 60% of the students have the correct address on record. What would be the 95% confidence interval of the true proportion of students who have failed to report a change of address?

A team of engineers at the research centre of a car manufacturer perform crash tests to determine the proportion of times the cars’ airbag fail to operate in a crash. With the airbag system’s new modified design, the team expected to reduce the failed proportion to below the last year’s proportion of 0.08. They decide to test H0 : p = 0.08 versus HA : p < 0.08, where p = the proportion of failed airbags during crash test. If 300 crashes performed in the lab resulted in 18 failures, which of the following is the test statistic for this test?
A grocer would like to determine the proportion of milk cartons that have expired within 0.05 of the true proportion with a 95% confidence interval. What is the minimum required sample size?

Researchers are concerned that the mean number of eggs per nest of robin’s eggs in a particular forest has decreased from the mean of 4.8 eggs measured 10 years ago. They plan to send out teams to locate and count the number of eggs in 50 different nests, and then perform a hypothesis test to determine if their concerns are well founded. What is the most appropriate test to perform in this situation?

options.text: A one-tailed z-test on one population proportion.
A one-tailed t-test on one population proportion.
A one-tailed z-test on one population mean.
A one-tailed t-test on one population mean.

questions.text: A farmer wants to know whether a new fertilizer has increased the mean weight of his apples. With the old fertilizer, the mean weight was 4 ounces per apple. The farmer decides to test H0 : \mu = 4 ounces versus HA : \mu > 4 ounces, at a 5 percent level of significance. The weights of apples are approximately normally distributed. The farmer takes a random sample of 16 apples and computes a mean of 4.3 ounces and a standard deviation of 0.6 ounces. Which of the following gives the p-value for this test?
Given the null hypothesis that all adults sleep 8 hours, which of the following is a Type I error?

options.text: Claiming all adults do not sleep 8 hours, when in fact, they do.
Failing to reject the claim that all adults sleep 8 hours when the statement is not true.
Claiming all adults sleep 8 hours when it is true.
Accepting the claim that all adults sleep 8 hours, when that statement is not true.

questions.text: Which of the following is true about the power of a test?

options.text: the power decreases as the sample size increases.
the power decreases as the effect size decreases.
the power decreases as the Type I error rate increases.
